# Design Once, Compose Many

**Flexibility: Suggested (MAY)**

## The Commitment

When designing governance objects, processes, or systems, participants MAY
invest in making them reusable and composable. A pattern designed once and
reused ten times costs less than ten bespoke solutions.

## What This Means

- Governance patterns that prove useful are extracted into reusable templates.
- Reusable components are registered in libraries for discovery.
- Composition is preferred over monolithic design where practical.
- Template libraries grow organically from proven patterns, not from speculative design.

## Violation Looks Like

Each venture reinvents the same governance structures from scratch. A proven
pattern exists in one context but is not available for reuse because it was
never extracted into a template.

## Derived From

- JOS-C20 (Composable Architecture) — the concept of composable design
- JOS-C12 (Volume Economics) — reuse amortizes design cost

### Framework Connection

Framework §7 (template/instance model) and §8 (slot fill model) provide the composition mechanism — a template designed once is instantiated many times, with slots filled per-context, making design-once-compose-many a structural capability rather than just an aspiration.
